title: Salted Caramel Bars
summary: Your snack drawer just levelled up. These savoury and sticky caramel bars are plant-based, freezer-friendly and far too easy to eat five at once!

method.description: Blitz the almonds in a food processor until crumbly - don’t go too fine, you still want some texture.
Chuck in the dates, almond butter, maca powder, vanilla extract and a pinch of salt. Blend again until it comes together into a sticky dough.
Line a loaf tin with baking parchment. Tip in the mixture and press it down firmly to an even layer.
Scatter over the extra nuts, if using, and press them gently into the surface.
Chill in the fridge until firm for at least an hour then slice into bars.
Once ready, wrap the bars in baking paper and chill or freeze for the ultimate grab-and-go snack. These last for a week in the fridge and 3 months in the freezer.

tips: Maca powder gives a natural caramel flavour, but if you don’t have any use caramel extract or leave it out entirely - it’s still delicious!
These work just as well with peanut or cashew butter.
Add a sprinkle of flaky sea salt on top if you’re after a salted caramel vibe.

chefs.name: Rachel Pamart
chefs.summary: Rachel is the founder of Rawlala, a cold-pressed juicery based in East London. She came to clean, holistic eating by tuning into what made her feel most alive and now she channels that into expert, ingredient-led recipes designed to nourish, energise, and taste damn good.
